data.title: Principal Financial Group Boosts Quarterly Dividend by 2.5%
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Principal Financial Group boosted its quarterly dividend 2.5% to 82 cents a share, up from 80 cents.

The new payout, equal to $3.28 a year, represents a 3.4% annual yield based on the company's Thursday closing price of $970.

The dividend will be paid on June 26 to shareholders of record as of June 1.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Ameriprise Financial Raises Quarterly Dividend 6%
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

Ameriprise Financial said its board has approved a 6% increase to the quarterly cash dividend.

The dividend will increase to $1.70 per share from $1.60. The dividend will be payable May 22 to shareholders of record at the end of business May 4.

The new dividend, $6.80 on an annual basis, represents a yield of 1.4%, based on Thursday's closing share price of $459.63 for Ameriprise.

Last April, the company raised its quarterly dividend to $1.60 per share from $1.48.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: NextNRG Secures Two $275,000 Convertible Notes From Agile Hudson and FirstFire
data.description:
NextNRG strengthened liquidity by closing two secured, convertible notes of $275,000 each with Agile Hudson and FirstFire, both carrying a $25,000 original issue discount and a one-time 10% interest charge. The notes mature in April 2027, convert at 80% of the average of the three lowest 15-day VWAPs (floor $0.10), and are secured by first-priority liens ranking pari passu with existing secured debt. Each counterparty received 50,000 commitment shares, participation and piggyback rights, MFN protections, and variable-rate issuance restrictions extend to 2027 or earlier payoff. Stock issuances above the Exchange Cap require shareholder approval.

Agreement 1: NextNRG Raises $275,000 Via Secured Convertible Note With Agile Hudson; $25,000 OID

  • Agreement type: Secured convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: Agile Hudson Partners
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 15 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until Apr 15 2027
  • Reason: Raise secured financing and enhance liquidity

Agreement 2: NextNRG Enters Securities Purchase Agreement With Agile Hudson to Support Financing

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement
  • Counterparty: Agile Hudson Partners
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 15 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Oct 15 2027 or until note repaid
  • Reason: Set financing terms and investor rights

Agreement 3: NextNRG Grants First-Priority Security Interest to Agile Hudson to Secure Note

  • Agreement type: First-priority security agreement (pari passu)
  • Counterparty: Agile Hudson Partners
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 15 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until obligations under note are satisfied
  • Reason: Secure obligations under the note

Agreement 4: NextNRG Raises $275,000 Via Secured Convertible Note With FirstFire; $25,000 OID

  • Agreement type: Secured convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: FirstFire Global Opportunities Fund
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until Apr 17 2027
  • Reason: Raise secured financing and enhance liquidity

Agreement 5: NextNRG Enters Securities Purchase Agreement With FirstFire to Support Financing

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement
  • Counterparty: FirstFire Global Opportunities Fund
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Oct 17 2027 or until note repaid
  • Reason: Set financing terms and investor rights

Agreement 6: NextNRG Grants First-Priority Security Interest to FirstFire to Secure Note

  • Agreement type: First-priority security agreement (pari passu)
  • Counterparty: FirstFire Global Opportunities Fund
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until obligations under note are satisfied
  • Reason: Secure obligations under the note

Original SEC Filing: NEXTNRG, INC. [ NXXT ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: Masimo to supplement proxy after shareholder suits; merger terms unchanged
data.description:
Masimo announced it will voluntarily supplement its definitive proxy statement for the Danaher merger after several stockholder challenges, while the merger consideration and meeting timing remain unchanged.

Key Highlights:

  • Masimo filed a definitive proxy for the May 1, 2026 virtual special meeting to vote on the Danaher merger.
  • Three shareholder lawsuits and additional demand letters allege the proxy is incomplete; plaintiffs seek injunctions, damages and fees.
  • Masimo denies the claims but will supplement disclosures to reduce litigation risk and potential delays to the merger.
  • Supplemental disclosures do not change the per-share merger consideration or the Board's recommendation to vote FOR the merger.

Original SEC Filing: MASIMO CORP [ MASI ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

data.title: Covenant Logistics Group reports Q1 2026: Revenue $307.2M, GAAP EPS $0.17, Adj. EPS $0.26
data.description:
Covenant Logistics Group reported first-quarter 2026 results with total revenue of $307.2 million and GAAP diluted earnings per share of $0.17 (adjusted diluted EPS $0.26). Net income was $4.42 million for the quarter, and adjusted net income was $6.92 million. The company cited severe weather and fuel cost headwinds in January and February and improving freight volumes and rates in March.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $307,161,000 for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Freight revenue (excluding fuel surcharge): $281,925,000.
  • Operating income (GAAP): $6,282,000; Adjusted operating income (non-GAAP): $9,610,000.
  • Net income (GAAP): $4,420,000; Adjusted net income (non-GAAP): $6,915,000.
  • Earnings per diluted share: $0.17 GAAP; Adjusted diluted EPS (non-GAAP): $0.26.

Business Highlights

  • Truckload operations: Combined truckload total revenue was $188.1 million; combined freight revenue was $163.0 million with segment operating income of $8.4 million.
  • Expedited segment: Freight revenue declined to $71.9 million; average tractors decreased by 88 units to 764 and average miles per tractor were lower versus prior year.
  • Dedicated segment: Freight revenue increased to $91.1 million driven by a 2.1% increase in average tractors to 1,510 and improved productivity in agricultural protein-related fleet.
  • Managed Freight: Freight revenue increased 59.6% year-over-year to $90.7 million, largely due to integration of assets acquired in Q4 2025; segment faced higher costs securing capacity.
  • Warehousing: Freight revenue increased to $27.6 million following onboarding of a significant new customer in Q4 2025; startup expenses temporarily offset margin improvement expectations.
  • Capital and fleet strategy: Net indebtedness decreased to ~$245.3 million; cash and cash equivalents were $11.2 million; average tractor age rose to 26 months and planned 2026 net capital equipment expenditures remain $40–$50 million.

Original SEC Filing: COVENANT LOGISTICS GROUP, INC. [ CVLG ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Intel Stock Soars on Strong Earnings and Strong Outlook — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Levine and Adam Clark

Intel shares were up sharply in after-hours trading Thursday after the chip maker reported strong first-quarter results.

Adjusted earnings-per-share were 29 cents, well ahead of Wall Street's consensus estimate of two cents, and up from 13 cents last year. Revenue for the quarter reached $13.6 billion, above expectations of $12.4 billion, and up 7% on the year.

Intel's guidance for the second quarter was also better than expected, with sales seen growing 11% at the midpoint of its forecasted range, and a much improved gross profit margin.

Intel overperformed in both of its end markets. Sales for PCs were not as badly impacted as expected by the memory shortage that is driving up prices and data center sales grew by 22%.

Intel's manufacturing segment, which still has a single internal customer for now, lost $2.4 billion. Intel hopes to change that after a new deal with Elon Musk's companies.

The company took a $4.1 billion restructuring charge in the quarter tied to a goodwill impairment at the company's Mobileye auto chip subsidiary.

The stock jumped by as much as 17% in after-hours trading following the report. A the recent after-hours price of $75.87, the stock would surpass its its record closing high of $74.88 from August 31, 2000.

It's been a long journey since then. The company had manufacturing technology problems for years, which led to a change in direction in 2021 under new CEO Pat Gelsinger. For the first time, the company decided to contract out its manufacturing like the leader in that business, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing. From the stock's 2020 peak, it fell to $17.67 a year ago. As late as last August, shares traded at $18.97.

But a new deal-making CEO, Lip-Bu Tan, has shaken up the narrative. First, he sold a 9% share of the company to the U.S. government, earning the favor of President Donald Trump. Then he formed a loose partnership with Nvidia, the AI-chip leader, that included a 4.5% stake in Intel for Nvidia.

The narrative was boosted in the past month by Intel buying back a portion of a factory it had previously sold to Apollo Global Management, as well as with a new deal with Elon Musk's companies to build a massive factory complex in Texas.

The stock recently trading at 92 times its projected earnings-per-share for the next 12 months, a record for the company. The broad S&P 500 index trades at around a 21 multiple to earnings.

Intel's major issue continues to be that its manufacturing technology lags Taiwan Semi, and that it has made it difficult to sign up external clients. Outside customers are key, because Tan has said that the next step in Intel's manufacturing technology will be too expensive to build without external income.

However, things are looking up. Tesla CEO Musk told analysts on Wednesday that he expects to use Intel's 14A manufacturing technology at the TeraFab manufacturing facility which will serve the electric-vehicle maker as well as SpaceX.

"We plan to use Intel's 14A process, which is state-of-the-art, and in fact not yet totally complete," Musk said. "But given that by the time TeraFab scales up, 14A will be probably [be] fairly mature or ready for primetime. 14A seems like the right move."

Still, 14A isn't expected to launch until 2028.

Intel has thus far missed out on much of the revenue generated by trillions of dollars being invested into AI data centers. The primary beneficiary has been Nvidia. In a two-way data center revenue race between the companies, Intel held a 71% share in 2021, which dwindled to 7% last year.

Write to Adam Levine at adam.levine@barrons.com and Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: International Seaways to Announce First Quarter 2026 Results on May 7, 2026
data.description:
NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 23, 2026--

International Seaways, Inc. (the "Company" or "INSW") announced today that it plans to release first quarter 2026 results before market open on Thursday, May 7, 2026. The Company will host a conference call for investors at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time ("ET") on the same day.

Conference Call Details:

 
Date:             Thursday, May 7, 2026 
Time              9:00 AM ET 
Dial-in Numbers   US: +1 (800) 715-9871 
                  International: +1 (646) 307-1963 
Conference ID     1842743

A live webcast of the conference call will be available from the Investor Relations section of the Company's website at https://www.intlseas.com/.

An audio replay of the conference call will be available starting at 12:00 p.m. ET on Thursday, May 7, 2026 through 11:59 p.m. ET on Thursday, May 14, 2026 by dialing +1 (800) 770-2030 for domestic callers and +1 (609) 800-9909 for international callers, and entering Access Code 1842743.

About International Seaways, Inc.

International Seaways, Inc. is one of the largest tanker companies worldwide providing energy transportation services for crude oil and petroleum products in International Flag markets. International Seaways owns and operates a fleet of approximately 70 vessels across the principal tanker asset classes including four vessels on order. International Seaways has an experienced team committed to the very best operating practices and the highest levels of customer service and operational efficiency. International Seaways is headquartered in New York City, NY. Additional information is available at https://www.intlseas.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

This release contains forward-looking statements. In addition, the Company may make or approve certain statements in future filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC"), in press releases, or in oral or written presentations by representatives of the Company. All statements other than statements of historical facts should be considered forward-looking statements. These matters or statements may relate to plans to issue dividends, the Company's prospects, including statements regarding vessel acquisitions, expected synergies, trends in the tanker markets, and possibilities of strategic alliances and investments. Forward-looking statements are based on the Company's current plans, estimates and projections, and are subject to change based on a number of factors. Investors should carefully consider the risk factors outlined in more detail in the Annual Report on Form 10-K for 2025 for the Company, and in similar sections of other filings made by the Company with the SEC from time to time. The Company assumes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements and written and oral forward-looking statements attributable to the Company or its representatives after the date of this release are qualified in their entirety by the cautionary statements contained in this paragraph and in other reports previously or hereafter filed by the Company with the SEC.

data.title: FDA Requests Additional Information on AbbVie's Application for Neurotoxin
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

The Food and Drug Administration requested further information regarding AbbVie's application for a neurotoxin aimed at facial aesthetics.

AbbVie said Thursday the FDA's complete response letter to its biologics license application for trenibotulinumtoxinE requested additional information about manufacturing processes. The letter didn't identify any safety or efficacy concerns for the neurotoxin, the company said.

AbbVie expects to submit a response in the coming months. The company said it believes trenibotulinumtoxinE has the potential to expand patients' options in facial aesthetics.

"Though disappointed, we remain confident in the strength and integrity of our application, and we are well positioned to respond to the agency's feedback promptly to support completion of the review," said Roopal Thakkar, executive vice president, research and development, chief scientific officer at AbbVie.

Regulatory reviews for the neurotoxin in other countries are ongoing and progressing as expected, AbbVie said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Duke Energy Gets 20-Year Extension for Nuclear Plant
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Duke Energy received a renewed operating license from regulators for its nuclear plant in South Carolina for an additional 20 years, extending the plant's operations until 2050.

The energy holding company said Thursday the extension, granted by the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission, is for its Robinson Nuclear Plant in Hartsville. The plant provides 759 megawatts of electricity, powering nearly 570,000 homes, the company said.

Robinson is the second Duke Energy nuclear facility to receive approval for subsequent license renewal, following Oconee Nuclear Station in 2025. The company said it plans to seek subsequent license renewal for all 11 operating units across its nuclear portfolio.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Baker Hughes beats first-quarter profit boosted by industrial, energy tech demand
data.description:
Oilfield services provider Baker Hughes surpassed Wall Street expectations for first-quarter profit on Thursday, helped by strength in its industrial and energy technology unit.

Growing electricity demand from data centers, long‑cycle gas projects and spending on gas turbines, compressors and grid‑related equipment are lending support to energy equipment providers like Baker Hughes.

First-quarter IET orders rose to $4.89 billion from last year's $3.18 billion.

However, Baker Hughes and peers are yet to benefit meaningfully from a surge in oil prices due to the disruptions caused by attacks on infrastructure in the Middle East and Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, as producers have taken a more cautious stance on boosting drilling.

The Houston-based company posted an adjusted profit of 58 cents per share for the three months ended March 31, compared with analysts' estimates of 49 cents per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Insperity Announces Updated Time for First Quarter Earnings Conference Call
data.description:
HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 23, 2026--

Insperity, Inc. , a leading provider of human resources and business performance solutions for America's best businesses, announced today that is has changed the time of its first quarter earnings conference call. The call will now take place at 5:00 p.m. ET instead of 8:30 a.m. ET on the same date (Thursday, April 30, 2026). All other details regarding the earnings release and conference call remain unchanged.

About Insperity

Since 1986, Insperity's mission has been to help businesses succeed so communities prosper. Offering a suite of the most comprehensive, scalable HR solutions available in the marketplace, Insperity is defined by an unrivaled breadth and depth of services and level of care. Through an optimal blend of premium HR service and technology, Insperity delivers the administrative relief, reduced liabilities and better benefit solutions that businesses need to drive performance and growth. With 2025 revenues of $6.8 billion and sales and service operations throughout the U.S., Insperity is currently making a difference in thousands of businesses and communities nationwide. data.title: Blue Ridge Bank reports Q1 2026 net income $0.8M, declares $0.60 special dividend
data.description:
Blue Ridge Bank reported net income of $0.8 million, or $0.01 per diluted common share, for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, marking the fourth consecutive profitable quarter as the company shifts back to a community banking model. Results included after-tax executive transition expenses of $1.3 million and an after-tax recovery of credit losses of $0.5 million; excluding transition expenses net income was $2.1 million, or $0.02 per diluted share. The company declared a special cash dividend of $0.60 per share (approximately $54.1 million) payable April 27, 2026, and amended warrants to adjust exercise price for dividends in lieu of cash distributions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $0.8 million for Q1 2026, or $0.01 per diluted common share; adjusted (ex-transition expenses) net income $2.1 million, or $0.02 per diluted common share.
  • Net interest income: $16.9 million for Q1 2026 (down from $18.1 million in Q4 2025).
  • Net interest margin (NIM): 2.90% for Q1 2026 (3.04% in prior quarter).
  • Noninterest expense: $18.7 million for Q1 2026 (increase of $1.8 million sequentially, driven by salaries and benefits including executive transition and incentive-related expenses).
  • Capital and dividends: Special cash dividend of $0.60 per common share (~$54.1 million) declared March 30, 2026; tangible common equity to tangible assets 11.4%; tangible book value per common share $3.11.

Business Highlights

  • Strategic shift to community banking: Continued reduction of non-strategic, out-of-market loans and winddown of indirect fintech lending partnerships; loans held for sale reduced to zero in the quarter.
  • Loan portfolio actions: Payoffs and paydowns included approximately $24.1 million of out-of-market loans in Q1; loans held for investment declined to $1.83 billion at March 31, 2026.
  • Deposit composition: Brokered deposits declined (to $207.2 million) as time deposits matured and were paid off; core (non-brokered) deposits increased $13.4 million sequentially.
  • Headcount and cost initiatives: Workforce reduced by 70 employees (20%) since Q1 2025 to 281 employees; ongoing noninterest expense reduction initiatives expected to yield benefits in H2 2026.
  • Regulatory status and operational focus: First full quarter following release from regulatory consent order (Nov 2025), enabling renewed focus on sound growth initiatives and capital flexibility used to fund the special dividend.

Original SEC Filing: BLUE RIDGE BANKSHARES, INC. [ BRBS ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: AngioDynamics sues Endovascular Engineering over alleged patent infringement of Hēlo system
data.description:
AngioDynamics filed a patent infringement lawsuit against Endovascular Engineering over alleged copying of its self‑expanding funnel technology.

Key Highlights:

  • Filed complaint in U.S. District Court for Delaware alleging E2’s Hēlo Thrombectomy System infringes AngioDynamics patents.
  • Claims the product incorporates features tied to AngioDynamics’ proprietary self‑expanding funnel tech that aid thrombus and embolus removal.
  • Seeking damages and equitable relief; company says enforcing IP supports innovation and patient care.
  • Notes litigation outcomes and costs are forward‑looking risks and could affect future results.

Original SEC Filing: ANGIODYNAMICS INC [ ANGO ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026
